name: get_user_using_email
description:
Retrieve a user by their email address.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific user using their email address as the identifier.
        The email must be in valid format (e.g., user@domain.com).
        
        Authentication Requirements:
          - x-itsm-user-token: Optional valid user authentication token. Validates the requester's identity.
          - API returns 401 Unauthorized if provided token is invalid.
        
        Parameters:
          - email (required): User's email address
        
        Returns a single user object if found, or an error if the user doesn't exist or email format is invalid.

name: get_user
description:
Retrieve a user by their user ID.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific user using their unique user_id identifier.
        
        Authentication Requirements:
          - x-itsm-user-token: Optional user authentication token. Validates the requester's identity.
          - API returns 401 Unauthorized if provided token is invalid.
        
        Parameters:
          - user_id (required): User's unique identifier (format: USER_001, USER_002, etc.)
        
        Returns a single user object if found, or an error if the user doesn't exist.

name: get_user_using_name
description:
Retrieve a user by their first and last name combination.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific user using both their first name and last name.
        The search is case-insensitive.
        
        Authentication Requirements:
          - x-itsm-user-token: Optional user authentication token. Validates the requester's identity.
          - API returns 401 Unauthorized if provided token is invalid.
        
        Parameters:
          - first_name (required): User's first name
          - last_name (required): User's last name
        
        Returns a single user object if found, or an error if no user matches the name combination.

name: list_users
description:
List users with optional filters.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve a list of users. Without filters, returns all users.
        With filters, returns only users matching the specified criteria.
        
        Authentication Requirements:
          - x-itsm-user-token: Optional user authentication token. Validates the requester's identity.
          - API returns 401 Unauthorized if provided token is invalid.
        
        Optional Filters:
          - email: Filter by exact email address
          - user_id: Filter by exact user ID
          - first_name: Filter by first name (case-insensitive, partial match)
          - last_name: Filter by last name (case-insensitive, partial match)
          - phone: Filter by phone number (format: +1-415-555-0101)
          - role: Filter by role (admin, manager, agent, reporter)
          - active: Filter by active status (true or false)
          - created_after: Filter users created after date (ISO 8601 format: 2024-01-15 or 2024-01-15T09:00:00)
          - created_before: Filter users created before date (ISO 8601 format)
        
        Returns a list of user objects matching the filters, or all users if no filters provided.

role.description: Filter by user role (admin, manager, agent, reporter)

created_after.description: Filter users created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter users created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: add_new_user
description:
Create a new user in the system.
        
        Use this tool to add a new user with the specified details. The system automatically generates
        user_id (incremental: USER_001, USER_002, etc.), user_name (from first.last), and other system fields.
        
        Authentication Requirements:
          - x-itsm-user-token: Optional user authentication token. Validates the requester's identity.
          - API returns 401 Unauthorized if provided token is invalid.
        
        Required Parameters:
          - first_name: User's first name (1-60 characters)
          - last_name: User's last name (1-60 characters)
          - email: User's email address (must be unique and valid format)
          - phone: User's phone number (format: +1-415-555-0101, must be unique)
          - role: User's role (must be one of: admin, manager, agent, reporter)
          - active: Whether the user account is active (true or false)
        
        Optional Parameters:
          - user_name: User login name (generated from first.last if not provided)
          - location_id: Location identifier
        
        Note: The organization ID (org_id) is automatically set from the authenticated user's organization.
        
        Auto-Generated Fields:
          - user_id: Incremental ID (USER_001, USER_002, etc.)
          - user_name: Generated from first_name.last_name (lowercase)
          - static_token: Secure authentication token
          - created_on and updated_on timestamps
          - sys_id and other audit fields
        
        Returns the newly created user object with all fields.

email.description: User's email address (valid email format required)

active.description: Whether the user account is active

user_name.description: User login name (generated from first.last if not provided)

name: update_user_details
description:
Update specific fields of an existing user.
        
        Use this tool to update one or more fields of a user identified by user_id.
        At least one field (besides user_id) must be provided. The tool validates that
        the new values are different from current values (idempotency check).
        
        Authentication Requirements:
          - x-itsm-user-token: Optional user authentication token. Validates the requester's identity.
          - API returns 401 Unauthorized if provided token is invalid.
        
        Required Parameter:
          - user_id: Unique identifier of the user to update
        
        Optional Parameters (at least one required):
          - email: Updated email address (must be unique and valid format)
          - first_name: Updated first name (1-60 characters)
          - last_name: Updated last name (1-60 characters)
          - active: Updated active status (true or false)
          - phone: Updated phone number (format: +1-415-555-0101, must be unique)
          - role: Updated role (must be one of: admin, manager, agent, reporter)
          - user_name: Updated user login name (must be unique)
          - company_id: Updated company identifier
          - location_id: Updated location identifier
        
        Note: Updating a field to its current value will result in an error (no changes detected).
        System fields like sys_id, created_on, etc. cannot be directly modified.
        
        Returns the updated user object with all fields.

user_id.description: User's unique identifier to identify which user to update

email.description: Updated email address (valid email format required)

user_name.description: Updated user login name (must be unique)

name: list_user_groups
description: List user groups with optional filters. Returns all user groups or filtered results based on provided criteria. Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

created_after.description: Filter groups created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter groups created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: find_group_by_name
description: Find a user group by its exact name. Returns the group details if found. Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

name.description: The exact name of the user group to find

name: add_new_user_group
description: Add a new user group to the system. Group name must be unique. Returns the newly created group object. Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

name: update_user_group
description: Update user group details. At least one field must be provided. Group name must be unique if provided. Returns error if all fields match current values (idempotency validation). Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

name: add_new_group_member
description: Add a new group member to a user group. Validates that group and user exist, and that the user is not already a member. Returns the newly created membership object. Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

name: remove_group_membership
description: Remove group membership from a user group. Validates that group, user, and optional member_id exist. Member_id must match the group_id and user_id if provided. Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

member_id.description: Member's unique identifier (optional for additional validation)

name: list_group_members
description: List group members with optional filters. Returns all group members or filtered results based on provided criteria. Requires x-itsm-user-token for authentication requirements. Returns 401 Unauthorized if token is invalid or missing. Response includes sys_* audit fields.

created_after.description: Filter members created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter members created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: find_location_by_given_name
description:
Find a location by its name.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific location using its name as the identifier.
        The search is case-sensitive and requires an exact name match.
        
        Parameters:
          - name (required): Location name to search for
        
        Returns a single location object if found, or an error if the location doesn't exist.

name.description: Name of the location to find

name: find_locations
description:
List locations with optional filters.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve a list of locations. Without filters, returns all locations.
        With filters, returns only locations matching the specified criteria.
        
        Optional Filters:
          - name: Filter by location name (partial match)
          - city: Filter by city (partial match)
          - country: Filter by country (partial match)
          - active: Filter by active status (true or false)
          - created_after: Filter locations created after date (ISO 8601 format: 2024-01-01 or 2024-01-01T08:00:00)
          - created_before: Filter locations created before date (ISO 8601 format)
        
        Returns a list of location objects matching the filters, or all locations if no filters provided.

created_after.description: Filter locations created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter locations created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: add_location
description:
Create a new location in the system.
        
        Use this tool to add a new location with the specified details. The system automatically generates
        location_id (incremental: LOC_001, LOC_002, etc.) and timestamps.
        
        Required Parameters:
          - name: Location name (must be unique)
          - city: City name (cannot be empty)
          - country: Country (cannot be empty)
        
        Optional Parameters:
          - active: Whether the location is active (default: true)
          - plot_no: Plot/building number
          - street: Street address
          - state: State/province/region
        
        Auto-Generated Fields:
          - location_id: Incremental ID (LOC_001, LOC_002, etc.)
          - created_on and updated_on timestamps
        
        Returns the newly created location object with all fields.

name: get_location_by_id
description:
Get a location by its ID.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific location using its unique location_id identifier.
        
        Parameters:
          - location_id (required): Location's unique identifier (format: LOC_001, LOC_002, etc.)
        
        Returns a single location object if found, or an error if the location doesn't exist.

name: update_location
description:
Update specific fields of an existing location.
        
        Use this tool to update one or more fields of a location identified by location_id.
        At least one field (besides location_id) must be provided. The tool validates that
        the new values are different from current values (idempotency check).
        
        Required Parameter:
          - location_id: Unique identifier of the location to update
        
        Optional Parameters (at least one required):
          - name: Updated location name (must be unique)
          - active: Updated active status
          - plot_no: Updated plot/building number
          - street: Updated street address
          - city: Updated city name (cannot be empty if provided)
          - state: Updated state/province/region
          - country: Updated country (cannot be empty if provided)
        
        Note: If updating city or country, they cannot be empty strings.
        
        Note: Updating a field to its current value will result in an error (no changes detected).
        
        Returns the updated location object with all fields.

location_id.description: Location's unique identifier to identify which location to update

city.description: Updated city name (cannot be empty if provided)

country.description: Updated country (cannot be empty if provided)

name: create_new_incident_template
description:
Create a new incident template in the system.
        
        Use this tool to add a new incident template with the specified details. The system automatically
        generates incident_template_id (incremental: TMPL_001, TMPL_002, etc.) and timestamps.
        
        Required Parameters:
          - name: Template name (1-120 characters, must be unique)
          - change_request_values: JSON object containing template fields
        
        Required fields inside change_request_values:
          - caller_id: User ID who is the default caller
          - short_description: Brief description (1-120 characters)
        
        Optional fields inside change_request_values:
          - channel: Incident channel (values: SELF_SERVICE, PHONE, EMAIL, CHAT, WALK_IN, VIRTUAL_AGENT)
          - category: Incident category (values: INQUIRY_HELP, SOFTWARE, HARDWARE, DATABASE, PASSWORD_RESET)
          - impact: Impact level (values: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) - defaults to LOW
          - urgency: Urgency level (values: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) - defaults to LOW
          - priority: Priority level (values: CRITICAL, HIGH, MODERATE, LOW, PLANNING) - defaults to PLANNING
          - configuration_item: Configuration item ID
          - service: Service ID
          - service_offering: Service offering ID
        
        Optional Parameters:
          - active: Whether the template is active (defaults to true)
        
        Auto-Generated Fields:
          - incident_template_id: Incremental ID (TMPL_001, TMPL_002, etc.)
          - created_at and updated_at timestamps
        
        Returns the newly created incident template object with all fields.

change_request_values.description: JSON object containing template fields. Required: caller_id, short_description. Optional: channel, category, impact, urgency, priority, configuration_item, service, service_offering

active.description: Whether the template is active (defaults to true)

name: update_incident_template
description:
Update specific fields of an existing incident template.
        
        Use this tool to update one or more fields of an incident template identified by incident_template_id.
        At least one field (besides incident_template_id) must be provided. The tool validates that
        the new values are different from current values (idempotency check).
        
        IMPORTANT - Field Update Behavior:
        - Empty strings ("") are IGNORED - the field will not be updated
        - Explicit null values for nullable fields (channel, category, configuration_item, service, service_offering) will SET the field to null
        - Only fields with actual values will be validated and updated
        - At least one field must be provided with a non-empty value
        
        Required Parameter:
          - incident_template_id: Unique identifier of the template to update
        
        Optional Parameters (at least one required):
          - name: Updated template name (1-120 characters, must be unique)
            * Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update
            * Valid string = update to this value
          - change_request_values: JSON object containing any of these fields:
            * caller_id: Updated caller user ID (empty string = ignore)
            * short_description: Updated brief description (empty string = ignore)
            * channel: Updated incident channel (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)
              - Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update
              - null = set to null (clear the field)
              - Valid value = update to this value
            * category: Updated incident category (inquiry-help, software, hardware, database, password-reset)
              - Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update
              - null = set to null (clear the field)
              - Valid value = update to this value
            * impact: Updated impact level (high, medium, low) (empty string = ignore)
            * urgency: Updated urgency level (high, medium, low) (empty string = ignore)
            * priority: Updated priority level (critical, high, moderate, low, planning) (empty string = ignore)
            * configuration_item: Updated configuration item ID
              - Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update
              - null = set to null (clear the field)
              - Valid ID = update to this value
            * service: Updated service ID
              - Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update
              - null = set to null (clear the field)
              - Valid ID = update to this value
            * service_offering: Updated service offering ID
              - Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update
              - null = set to null (clear the field)
              - Valid ID = update to this value
          - active: Updated active status
            * null = ignore, don't update
            * true/false = update to this value
        
        Note: Updating a field to its current value will result in an error (no changes detected).
        
        Returns the updated incident template object with all fields.

incident_template_id.description: Unique identifier of the incident template to update

name.description: Updated template name (1-120 characters, must be unique). Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update

change_request_values.description: JSON object containing template fields to update. Empty strings ("") = ignore field. null for nullable fields = set to null. Can include: caller_id, short_description, channel, category, impact, urgency, priority, configuration_item, service, service_offering

caller_id.description: Updated caller user ID. Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update

short_description.description: Updated brief description. Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update

channel.description: Updated incident channel. Empty string ("") = ignore, null = set to null, valid value = update

category.description: Updated incident category. Empty string ("") = ignore, null = set to null, valid value = update

impact.description: Updated impact level. Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update

urgency.description: Updated urgency level. Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update

priority.description: Updated priority level. Empty string ("") = ignore, don't update

configuration_item.description: Updated configuration item ID. Empty string ("") = ignore, null = set to null, valid ID = update

service.description: Updated service ID. Empty string ("") = ignore, null = set to null, valid ID = update

service_offering.description: Updated service offering ID. Empty string ("") = ignore, null = set to null, valid ID = update

active.description: Updated active status. null = ignore, don't update. true/false = update to this value

name: get_incident_template_by_name
description:
Retrieve an incident template by its exact name.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific incident template using its name as the identifier.
        The search is case-sensitive and requires an exact match.
        
        Parameters:
          - name (required): Incident template name
        
        Returns a single incident template object if found, or an error if the template doesn't exist.

name: get_incident_templates
description:
List incident templates with optional filters.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve a list of incident templates. Without filters, returns all templates.
        With filters, returns only templates matching the specified criteria.
        
        Optional Filters:
          - incident_template_id: Filter by exact template ID
          - name: Filter by template name (exact match)
          - active: Filter by active status (boolean: true or false)
          - created_after: Filter templates created after date (ISO 8601 format: 2024-01-15 or 2024-01-15T09:00:00)
          - created_before: Filter templates created before date (ISO 8601 format)
        
        Returns a list of incident template objects matching the filters, or all templates if no filters provided.

incident_template_id.description: Filter by incident template ID (exact match)

active.description: Filter by active status (true or false)

created_after.description: Filter templates created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter templates created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: register_configuration_item
description:
Register a new configuration item in the system.
        
        Use this tool to add a new configuration item (CI) with the specified details.
        The system automatically generates configuration_item_id (CI_001, CI_002, etc.) and timestamps.
        
        Required Parameters:
          - name: CI name (1-120 characters, required)
          - owner_id: User ID who owns the CI
          - location_id: Location ID where CI is located
          - serial_number: Serial number (must be unique, 1-60 characters)
          - status: CI status (values: IN_USE, IN_STOCK, MAINTENANCE, RETIRED, DISPOSED)
          - cost: Cost of the CI (must be >= 0)
        
        Auto-Generated Fields:
          - configuration_item_id: Incremental ID (CI_001, CI_002, etc.)
          - created_on and updated_on timestamps
        
        Unique Constraints:
          - name must be unique across all CIs
          - serial_number must be unique across all CIs
          - Combination of (owner_id, serial_number, location_id) must be unique
        
        Returns the newly created configuration item object with all fields.

owner_id.description: User ID who owns the CI

location_id.description: Location ID where CI is located

status.description: CI status (in_use, in_stock, maintenance, retired, disposed)

name: update_configuration_item
description:
Update specific fields of an existing configuration item.
        
        Use this tool to update one or more fields of a CI identified by configuration_item_id.
        At least one field (besides configuration_item_id) must be provided. The tool validates that
        the new values are different from current values (idempotency check).
        
        Required Parameter:
          - configuration_item_id: Unique identifier of the CI to update
        
        Optional Parameters (at least one required):
          - owner_id: Updated owner user ID
          - location_id: Updated location ID
          - serial_number: Updated serial number (must remain unique)
          - status: Updated CI status (IN_USE, IN_STOCK, MAINTENANCE, RETIRED, DISPOSED)
          - cost: Updated cost (must be >= 0)
          - name: Updated CI name
        
        Unique Constraints:
          - If serial_number is updated, it must be unique
          - If owner_id, serial_number, or location_id are updated, the combination must be unique
        
        Note: Updating a field to its current value will result in an error (no changes detected).
        
        Returns the updated configuration item object with all fields.

configuration_item_id.description: Unique identifier of the CI to update

status.description: Updated CI status (in_use, in_stock, maintenance, retired, disposed)

name: find_configuration_item_by_serial_number
description:
Retrieve a configuration item by its serial number.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific CI using its serial number as the identifier.
        The serial number is unique across all configuration items.
        
        Parameters:
          - serial_number (required): Configuration item serial number
        
        Returns a single configuration item object if found, or an error if the CI doesn't exist.

name: find_configuration_items
description:
List configuration items with optional filters.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve a list of configuration items. Without filters, returns all CIs.
        With filters, returns only CIs matching the specified criteria.
        
        Optional Filters:
          - configuration_item_id: Filter by CI ID (exact match)
          - owner_id: Filter by owner user ID (exact match)
          - location_id: Filter by location ID (exact match)
          - serial_number: Filter by serial number (exact match)
          - status: Filter by status (IN_USE, IN_STOCK, MAINTENANCE, RETIRED, DISPOSED)
          - cost: Filter by cost (exact match)
          - created_after: Filter CIs created after date (ISO 8601 format: 2024-01-15 or 2024-01-15T09:00:00)
          - created_before: Filter CIs created before date (ISO 8601 format)
        
        Returns a list of configuration item objects matching the filters, or all CIs if no filters provided.

configuration_item_id.description: Filter by configuration item ID (exact match)

owner_id.description: Filter by owner user ID (exact match)

status.description: Filter by status

created_after.description: Filter CIs created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter CIs created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: find_services
description: List services with optional filters. Returns all services or filtered results.

status.description: Filter by status

created_after.description: Filter services created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter services created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: find_service_by_name
description: Find a service by its exact name. Returns the service details if found.

name.description: The exact name of the service to find

name: add_new_service
description: Add a new service to the system. Service name must be unique. Returns the newly created service object.

status.description: Service status

name: update_service
description: Update service details. At least one field must be provided. Service name must be unique if provided. Returns error if all fields match current values (idempotency validation).

status.description: Updated status

name: create_change
description:
Create a new change request.

Automatically generates:
- change_id (CHG_001, CHG_002, ...)
- number (CHG0000001, CHG0000002, ...)

Required fields:
- short_description: Brief description (<=120 chars)
- status: Change status (new, assess, authorize, scheduled, implement, review, closed, canceled)
- impact: Impact level (high, medium, low)
- risk: Risk level (high, medium, low)
- priority: Priority level (critical, high, moderate, low)

Optional fields:
- service: Service ID
- service_offering: Service offering ID
- configuration_item: Configuration item ID
- category: Change category (network, software, hardware, documentation, system_software, application_software, service, telecom, other)
- description: Detailed description (<=120 chars)
- implementation_plan: Implementation plan (<=120 chars)
- testing_plan: Testing plan (<=120 chars)
- cab_required: Boolean indicating if CAB approval is needed
- assigned_to: Assigned user ID
- assignment_group: Assigned group ID
- close_code: Close code (successful, successful_with_issues, unsuccessful)
- close_notes: Close notes (<=120 chars)

Example payload:
{
    "short_description": "Upgrade database cluster",
    "status": "assess",
    "impact": "high",
    "risk": "high",
    "priority": "critical",
    "category": "service",
    "implementation_plan": "Rollover to replica, upgrade primary, fail back"
}

status.description: Change status (new, assess, authorize, scheduled, implement, review, closed, canceled)

category.description: Change category (network, software, hardware, documentation, system_software, application_software, service, telecom, other)

name: update_change
description:
Update an existing change request by change_id.

All fields are optional; only provided fields are updated.
If no changes are detected, the API returns an error.

Example payload:
{
    "change_id": "CHG_001",
    "status": "implement",
    "assigned_to": "USER_010",
    "implementation_plan": "Deploy patch to production nodes sequentially"
}

change_id.description: Change ID (CHG_001, CHG_002, ...). Required when calling this tool.

requested_by.description: User ID who requested the change

category.description: Change category (network, software, hardware, documentation, system_software, application_software, service, telecom, other)

status.description: Change status (new, assess, authorize, scheduled, implement, review, closed, canceled)

name: find_change_by_number
description: Retrieve a change by its unique number (CHG0000001, CHG0000002, ...).

name: list_changes
description:
List changes applying optional filters. Returns all changes if no filters provided.

Supported filters (all optional):
- change_id, number, service, service_offering, configuration_item
- requested_by, assigned_to, assignment_group
- status, category, impact, risk, priority, close_code
- short_description, description, implementation_plan, testing_plan, close_notes (partial match)
- created_after, created_before (ISO timestamp strings)

status.description: Filter by status (new, assess, authorize, scheduled, implement, review, closed, canceled)

category.description: Filter by category (network, software, hardware, documentation, system_software, application_software, service, telecom, other)

priority.description: Filter by priority (critical, high, moderate, low)

close_code.description: Filter by close code (successful, successful_with_issues, unsuccessful)

created_after.description: ISO timestamp: return changes created after this value

created_before.description: ISO timestamp: return changes created before this value

name: get_changes_assigned_to
create_problem
description:
Create a new problem record.

Auto-generates:
- problem_id: PRB_001, PRB_002, etc.
- number: PRB0000001, PRB0000002, etc.

Required fields:
- problem_statement: Detailed problem statement
- status: Problem status (NEW, ASSESS, ROOT_CAUSE, FIX_IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, CLOSED)
- impact: Impact level (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
- urgency: Urgency level (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
- priority: Priority level (CRITICAL, HIGH, MODERATE, LOW, PLANNING)

Optional fields:
- short_description: Short description (max 255 chars)
- service: Service ID
- service_offering: Service offering ID
- configuration_item: Configuration item ID
- assigned_to: User ID assigned to
- assignment_group: Group ID assigned to
- original_task: Original incident ID
- category: Problem category (NETWORK, SOFTWARE, HARDWARE, DATABASE)
- worknotes: Work notes
- workaround: Workaround description
- fix_notes: Fix notes

Example:
{
    "problem_statement": "Multiple users experiencing periodic email service disruptions",
    "status": "NEW",
    "impact": "HIGH",
    "urgency": "MEDIUM",
    "priority": "HIGH",
    "category": "NETWORK",
    "short_description": "Email service outages"
}

status.description: Problem status (new, assess, root_cause, fix_in_progress, resolved, closed)

priority.description: Priority level (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)

name: update_problem
description:
Update an existing problem by problem_id or number.

Can update by:
- problem_id: PRB_001, PRB_002, etc.
- number: PRB0000001, PRB0000002, etc.

All fields are optional. Only provided fields will be updated.
Returns error if no changes detected (idempotency).

Example:
{
    "problem_id": "PRB_001",
    "status": "RESOLVED",
    "fix_notes": "Load balancer configuration corrected"
}

Or update by number:
{
    "number": "PRB0000001",
    "assigned_to": "USER_007"
}

problem_id.description: Problem ID (e.g., PRB_001). Use either this or 'number', not both.

number.description: Problem number (e.g., PRB0000001). Use either this or 'problem_id', not both.

opened_by.description: User ID who opened the problem

status.description: Problem status (new, assess, root_cause, fix_in_progress, resolved, closed)

priority.description: Priority level (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)

name: find_problem_by_number
list_problems
description:
List problems with optional filters.

All filters are optional. Returns all problems if no filters provided.

Supported filters:
- problem_id: Exact match on problem ID
- number: Exact match on number
- service: Exact match on service ID
- service_offering: Exact match on service offering ID
- configuration_item: Exact match on configuration item ID
- opened_by: Exact match on user who opened
- status: Exact match on status (NEW, ASSESS, ROOT_CAUSE, FIX_IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, CLOSED)
- category: Exact match on category (NETWORK, SOFTWARE, HARDWARE, DATABASE)
- problem_statement: Partial match on problem statement
- short_description: Partial match on short description
- impact: Exact match on impact (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
- urgency: Exact match on urgency (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
- priority: Exact match on priority (CRITICAL, HIGH, MODERATE, LOW, PLANNING)
- assigned_to: Exact match on assigned user
- assignment_group: Exact match on assigned group
- worknotes: Partial match on worknotes
- workaround: Partial match on workaround
- fix_notes: Partial match on fix notes
- original_task: Exact match on original incident ID
- created_after: Filter by created after datetime (ISO format)
- created_before: Filter by created before datetime (ISO format)

Example:
{
    "status": "NEW",
    "priority": "HIGH"
}

Returns:
{
    "problems": [...],
    "total_count": 5
}

status.description: Filter by status (new, assess, root_cause, fix_in_progress, resolved, closed)

category.description: Filter by category (network, software, hardware, database)

priority.description: Filter by priority (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)

name: get_problems_assigned_to
find_incident_by_number
description:
Retrieve an incident by its incident number.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific incident using its unique incident number.
        
        Parameters:
          - number (required): Incident number (e.g., INC-000001)
        
        Returns a single incident object if found, or an error if the incident doesn't exist.

name: find_incident_by_id
description:
Retrieve an incident by its incident ID.
        
        Use this tool to find a specific incident using its unique incident ID.
        
        Parameters:
          - incident_id (required): Incident's unique identifier
        
        Returns a single incident object if found, or an error if the incident doesn't exist.

name: list_incidents
description:
List incidents with optional filters.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve a list of incidents. Without filters, returns all incidents.
        With filters, returns only incidents matching the specified criteria.
        
        Optional Filters:
          - incident_id: Filter by exact incident ID
          - number: Filter by exact incident number
          - service: Filter by service ID
          - service_offering: Filter by service offering ID
          - configuration_item: Filter by configuration item ID
          - called_id: Filter by caller ID
          - channel: Filter by channel (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)
          - status: Filter by status (new, in_progress, on_hold, resolved, closed, canceled)
          - category: Filter by category (inquiry-help, software, hardware, database, password-reset, network)
          - short_description: Filter by short description (partial match)
          - description: Filter by description (partial match)
          - impact: Filter by impact (high, medium, low)
          - urgency: Filter by urgency (high, medium, low)
          - priority: Filter by priority (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)
          - assigned_to: Filter by assigned user ID
          - assignment_group: Filter by assignment group ID
          - worknotes: Filter by worknotes (partial match)
          - resolution_notes: Filter by resolution notes (partial match)
          - close_notes: Filter by close notes (partial match)
          - resolution_code: Filter by resolution code
          - parent_incident: Filter by parent incident ID
          - problem: Filter by problem ID
          - change_request: Filter by change request ID
          - caused_by_change: Filter by caused by change ID
          - resolved_by: Filter by resolved by user ID
          - on_hold_reason: Filter by on hold reason
          - incident_template: Filter by incident template ID
          - resolved: Filter by resolved timestamp
          - created_after: Filter incidents created after date (ISO 8601 format)
          - created_before: Filter incidents created before date (ISO 8601 format)
        
        Returns a list of incident objects matching the filters, or all incidents if no filters provided.

channel.description: Filter by channel (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)

status.description: Filter by status (new, in_progress, on_hold, resolved, closed, canceled)

category.description: Filter by category (inquiry-help, software, hardware, database, password-reset)

priority.description: Filter by priority (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)

caused_by_change.description: Filter by caused by change ID

resolved_by.description: Filter by resolved by user ID

created_after.description: Filter incidents created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter incidents created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: get_incidents_assigned_to
description:
Find incidents that are assigned to a particular group or to a user.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve incidents assigned to a specific user or group.
        
        Note: This tool requires incident CRUD permissions. Reporter roles do not have access
        to this tool, and agent roles must have write permissions to incidents.
        
        Parameters:
          - assignment_group (optional): Group ID to filter by
          - assigned_to (optional): User ID to filter by
        
        Returns a list of incident objects assigned to the specified group or user.

assignment_group.description: Group ID to filter incidents by

assigned_to.description: User ID to filter incidents by

name: create_incident
description:
Create a new incident in the system.
        
        Use this tool to create a new incident with the specified details.
        
        Required Parameters:
          - short_description: Brief description of the incident
          - caller_id: ID of the user who reported the incident
        
        Optional Parameters (all can be null/omitted):
          - service: Service ID related to the incident
          - service_offering: Service offering ID related to the incident
          - configuration_item: Configuration item ID related to the incident
          - channel: How the incident was reported (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)
          - status: Current status of the incident (new, in_progress, on_hold, resolved, closed, canceled). Default: "new"
          - category: Category of the incident (inquiry-help, software, hardware, database, password-reset, network). Default: "inquiry-help"
          - description: Detailed description of the incident
          - impact: Impact level (high, medium, low). Default: "low"
          - urgency: Urgency level (high, medium, low). Default: "low"
          - priority: Priority level (critical, high, moderate, low, planning). Default: "planning"
          - assigned_to: User ID to assign the incident to
          - assignment_group: Group ID to assign the incident to
          - worknotes: Work notes for the incident
          - resolution_notes: Resolution notes for the incident
          - close_notes: Close notes for the incident
          - resolution_code: Resolution code for the incident
          - parent_incident: Parent incident ID if this is a child incident
          - problem: Problem ID related to the incident
          - change_request: Change request ID related to the incident
          - caused_by_change: Change ID that caused this incident
          - resolved_by: User ID who resolved the incident
          - on_hold_reason: Reason why the incident is on hold
          - incident_template: Incident template ID to use
          - resolved: Timestamp when the incident was resolved
        
        Default Values:
          When a parameter is null or omitted, the following defaults are applied:
          - status: "new"
          - category: "inquiry-help"
          - impact: "low"
          - urgency: "low"
          - priority: "planning"
        
        Auto-Generated Fields:
          - incident_id: Unique identifier for the incident (format: INC_001, INC_002, etc.)
          - number: Incident number (e.g., INC-000001)
          - created_at: Timestamp when the incident was created
          - updated_at: Timestamp when the incident was last updated
        
        Returns the newly created incident object with all fields.

service.description: Service ID related to the incident

service_offering.description: Service offering ID related to the incident

configuration_item.description: Configuration item ID related to the incident

caller_id.description: ID of the user who reported the incident

channel.description: How the incident was reported (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)

status.description: Current status of the incident. Default: 'new' if null or omitted

category.description: Category of the incident. Default: 'inquiry-help' if null or omitted

impact.description: Impact level. Default: 'low' if null or omitted

urgency.description: Urgency level. Default: 'low' if null or omitted

priority.description: Priority level. Default: 'planning' if null or omitted

assigned_to.description: User ID to assign the incident to

assignment_group.description: Group ID to assign the incident to

parent_incident.description: Parent incident ID if this is a child incident

problem.description: Problem ID related to the incident

change_request.description: Change request ID related to the incident

caused_by_change.description: Change ID that caused this incident

resolved_by.description: User ID who resolved the incident

on_hold_reason.description: Reason why the incident is on hold

resolved.description: Timestamp when the incident was resolved (ISO 8601 format)

name: update_incident
description:
Update an existing incident in the system.
        
        Use this tool to update an existing incident with the specified details.
        
        Required Parameters:
          - incident_id: ID of the incident to update
        
        Optional Parameters:
          - number: Incident number
          - service: Service ID related to the incident
          - service_offering: Service offering ID related to the incident
          - configuration_item: Configuration item ID related to the incident
          - caller_id: ID of the user who reported the incident
          - channel: How the incident was reported (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)
          - status: Current status of the incident (new, in_progress, on_hold, resolved, closed, canceled)
          - category: Category of the incident (inquiry-help, software, hardware, database, password-reset, network)
          - short_description: Brief description of the incident
          - description: Detailed description of the incident
          - impact: Impact level (high, medium, low)
          - urgency: Urgency level (high, medium, low)
          - priority: Priority level (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)
          - assigned_to: User ID to assign the incident to
          - assignment_group: Group ID to assign the incident to
          - worknotes: Work notes for the incident
          - resolution_notes: Resolution notes for the incident
          - close_notes: Close notes for the incident
          - resolution_code: Resolution code for the incident
          - parent_incident: Parent incident ID if this is a child incident
          - problem: Problem ID related to the incident
          - change_request: Change request ID related to the incident
          - caused_by_change: Change ID that caused this incident
          - resolved_by: User ID who resolved the incident
          - on_hold_reason: Reason why the incident is on hold
          - incident_template: Incident template ID to use
          - resolved: Timestamp when the incident was resolved
        
        Returns the updated incident object with all fields.

incident_id.description: ID of the incident to update

service.description: Service ID related to the incident

service_offering.description: Service offering ID related to the incident

configuration_item.description: Configuration item ID related to the incident

caller_id.description: ID of the user who reported the incident

channel.description: How the incident was reported (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent)

status.description: Current status of the incident

assigned_to.description: User ID to assign the incident to

assignment_group.description: Group ID to assign the incident to

parent_incident.description: Parent incident ID if this is a child incident

problem.description: Problem ID related to the incident

change_request.description: Change request ID related to the incident

caused_by_change.description: Change ID that caused this incident

resolved_by.description: User ID who resolved the incident

on_hold_reason.description: Reason why the incident is on hold

resolved.description: Timestamp when the incident was resolved (ISO 8601 format)

name: list_child_incidents
description:
List child incidents for a parent incident.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve all child incidents associated with a parent incident.
        
        Parameters:
          - parent_incident (required): Parent incident ID
        
        Returns a list of child incident mapping objects.

name: find_parent_incident
description:
Find the parent incident for a child incident.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve the parent incident mapping for a child incident.
        
        Parameters:
          - child_incident (required): Child incident ID
        
        Returns the parent incident mapping object if found.

name: add_child_incident
description:
Add a child incident to a parent incident.
        
        Use this tool to create a parent-child relationship between two incidents.
        
        Parameters:
          - parent_incident (required): Parent incident ID
          - child_incident (required): Child incident ID
        
        Returns the child incident mapping object that was created.

name: add_child_incidents
description:
Add multiple child incidents to a parent incident.
        
        Use this tool to create parent-child relationships between a parent incident and multiple child incidents.
        
        Parameters:
          - parent_incident (required): Parent incident ID
          - child_incidents (required): Array of child incident IDs
        
        Returns a list of child incident mapping objects that were created.

name: update_child_incident
description:
Update a child incident mapping.
        
        Use this tool to update an existing parent-child relationship between incidents.
        
        Parameters:
          - child_incident_mapping_id (required): ID of the child incident mapping to update
          - parent_incident (required): Parent incident ID
          - child_incident (required): Child incident ID
        
        Returns the updated child incident mapping object.

child_incident_mapping_id.description: ID of the child incident mapping to update

name: remove_child_incident
description:
Remove a child incident mapping.
        
        Use this tool to remove a parent-child relationship between incidents.
        
        Parameters (at least one required):
          - child_incident_mapping_id: ID of the child incident mapping to remove
          - parent_incident AND child_incident: Parent and child incident IDs to remove the mapping for
        
        Returns a boolean indicating success.

child_incident_mapping_id.description: ID of the child incident mapping to remove

name: get_count_of_incident_priority_wise
description:
Get count of incidents grouped by priority.
        
        Returns an Object with priority as the key and the count of incidents which has that priority value
        as the value, for example {'critical': 5, 'low': 3}.
        
        Parameters:
          - priority_list (optional): Array of priorities to filter by (e.g., ["critical", "high", "low"])
        
        If a priority list is given in input then only those priorities will be considered for output.
        Empty string values in the array are ignored. All values must be valid Priority enum values.

priority_list.description: Optional array of priorities to filter. Valid values: critical, high, moderate, low, planning. Empty strings are ignored.

name: count_incident_for_assignment_group
description:
Count incidents for a specific assignment group with optional filters.
        
        Returns object where group id is the key and the number of incidents assigned to it is the count value.
        
        Parameters:
          - assignment_group_id (required): The assignment group ID to count incidents for
          - status (optional): Filter by incident status (new, in_progress, on_hold, resolved, closed, canceled)
          - priority (optional): Filter by incident priority (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)
          - channel (optional): Filter by incident channel (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent, web)
        
        Optional fields are to support filtering to only find specific values.
        Empty string values are treated as no filter. All values must be valid enum values.

assignment_group_id.description: The assignment group ID to count incidents for

status.description: Optional status filter (new, in_progress, on_hold, resolved, closed, canceled)

priority.description: Optional priority filter (critical, high, moderate, low, planning)

channel.description: Optional channel filter (self-service, phone, email, chat, walk-in, virtual-agent, web)

name: find_service_offerings
description: List service offerings with optional filters. Returns all service offerings or filtered results.

service_offering_id.description: Filter by service offering ID (exact match)

name.description: Filter by service offering name (partial match)

status.description: Filter by status

created_after.description: Filter service offerings created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter service offerings created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: find_service_offering_by_name
description: Find a service offering by its exact name. Returns the service offering details if found.

name.description: The exact name of the service offering to find

name: register_new_service_offering
description: Register a new service offering to the system. Service offering name must be unique. Returns the newly created service offering object.

status.description: Service offering status

short_description.description: Short description of the service offering

name: update_service_offering
description: Update service offering details. At least one field must be provided. Service offering name must be unique if provided. Returns error if all fields match current values (idempotency validation).

status.description: Updated status

name: retrieve_knowledge_articles
description:
List knowledge articles with optional filters.
        
        Use this tool to retrieve a list of knowledge articles. Without filters, returns all articles.
        With filters, returns only articles matching the specified criteria.
        
        Returns a list of knowledge article objects matching the filters with total_count, or all articles if no filters provided.

knowledge_id.description: Filter by knowledge ID (exact match, e.g., KB_001)

kb_number.description: Filter by KB number (exact match, e.g., KB0000001)

body.description: Filter by body content (partial match, case-insensitive)

owner_id.description: Filter by owner user ID (exact match)

created_after.description: Filter articles created after this date (ISO 8601 format)

created_before.description: Filter articles created before this date (ISO 8601 format)

name: create_knowledge_article
description:
Create a new knowledge article in the system.
        
        Use this tool to add a new knowledge article with the specified details. The system automatically
        generates knowledge_id (KB_001, KB_002, etc.), kb_number (KB0000001, KB0000002, etc.), and timestamps.
        Returns the newly created knowledge article object with all fields.

body.description: Full article content (detailed explanation, solutions, troubleshooting steps, etc.)

owner_id.description: User ID who owns the article (author/responsible person)

name: update_knowledge_article
description:
Update a knowledge article by either its knowledge_id OR kb_number.
        
        Use this tool to update one or more fields of a knowledge article. You can identify the article
        using either knowledge_id (e.g., KB_001) or kb_number (e.g., KB0000001). These are mutually
        exclusive identifiers - provide exactly one, not both.
        
        Returns the updated knowledge article object with all fields.

knowledge_id.description: Unique identifier of the article to update (e.g., KB_001). Mutually exclusive with kb_number - provide only one.

kb_number.description: Unique KB number of the article to update (e.g., KB0000001). Mutually exclusive with knowledge_id - provide only one.

name: find_incident_knowledge_links
description: Find incident-knowledge links with optional filters. Returns list of incident knowledge links matching the criteria.

incident_kb_id.description: Filter by incident knowledge link ID

used_as.description: Filter by usage type(s). Can be a single value or comma-separated list of values (e.g., 'suggested,applied,resolution' or 'suggested, applied, resolution'). Valid values (lowercase only): suggested, applied, resolution. Returns links matching any of the specified values. Values must be provided in lowercase.

name: link_knowledge_to_incident
description: Link a knowledge article to an incident. Creates a new incident-knowledge link.

incident_id.description: Incident ID to link knowledge to

name: remove_knowledge_link_to_incident
description: Remove a knowledge link from an incident. Can identify the link by incident_kb_id OR by combination of incident_id and knowledge_id.

incident_kb_id.description: Incident knowledge link ID to remove

incident_id.description: Incident ID (must be used with knowledge_id)

knowledge_id.description: Knowledge article ID (must be used with incident_id)

name: add_new_sla_definition
description:
Create a new SLA definition.

Automatically generates:
- sla_def_id (SLA_001, SLA_002, ...)

Required fields:
- name: Unique name for the SLA definition (<=120 chars)
- metric: response or resolution
- target_mins: Target time in minutes (>=1)
- pause_on_pending: Boolean indicating if SLA pauses on pending status
- active: Boolean indicating if the SLA definition is active

Optional fields:
- applies_to_priority: CRITICAL, HIGH, MODERATE, LOW
- schedule: Schedule ID

Example payload:
{
    "name": "Critical Response SLA",
    "metric": "RESPONSE",
    "target_mins": 60,
    "pause_on_pending": true,
    "active": true,
    "applies_to_priority": "CRITICAL"
}

name.description: Name of the SLA definition (unique, <=120 chars)

pause_on_pending.description: Whether to pause SLA on pending status

active.description: Whether the SLA definition is active

name: update_sla_definition
description:
Update an existing SLA definition by sla_def_id.

All fields are optional; only provided fields are updated.
If no changes are detected, the API returns an error.

Example payload:
{
    "sla_def_id": "SLA_001",
    "target_mins": 120,
    "active": false
}

sla_def_id.description: SLA definition ID (SLA_001, SLA_002, ...). Required when calling this tool.

name.description: Name of the SLA definition (unique, <=120 chars)

pause_on_pending.description: Whether to pause SLA on pending status

active.description: Whether the SLA definition is active

name: find_sla_definition_by_name
find_sla_definitions
description:
List SLA definitions applying optional filters. Returns all SLA definitions if no filters provided.

Supported filters (all optional):
- sla_def_id, name
- metric (RESPONSE, RESOLUTION)
- target_mins, pause_on_pending, active
- applies_to_priority (CRITICAL, HIGH, MODERATE, LOW)
- created_after, created_before (ISO timestamp strings)

Note:
- Empty strings ("") or null values for parameters will return all results without filtering
- For boolean fields (pause_on_pending, active), only use true/false to filter - null will return all results

Example:
{
    "metric": "RESPONSE",
    "active": true,
    "applies_to_priority": "CRITICAL"
}

metric.description: Filter by metric (response or resolution). Null returns all.

pause_on_pending.description: Filter by pause_on_pending. Null returns all.

applies_to_priority.description: Filter by applies_to_priority. Null returns all.

active.description: Filter by active status. Null returns all.

created_after.description: ISO timestamp: return SLA definitions created after this value

created_before.description: ISO timestamp: return SLA definitions created before this value

name: total_sum_of_target_mins_per_priority
description:
Compute the total sum of target minutes grouped by priority.

Optional arguments:
- priority: Array of priorities to filter by (critical, high, moderate, low)

Returns an object where keys are priorities and values are summed target minutes.
Example response:
{
    "CRITICAL": 120,
    "HIGH": 90,
    "MODERATE": 60
}

priority.description: Optional list of priorities to filter by. Accepted values: critical, high, moderate, low.

name: average_target_mins_per_priority
description:
Compute the average of target minutes grouped by priority.

Optional arguments:
- priority: Array of priorities to filter by (critical, high, moderate, low)

Returns an object where keys are priorities and values are average target minutes.
Values are rounded to 2 decimal places.
Example response:
{
    "CRITICAL": 45.5,
    "HIGH": 30.0
}

priority.description: Optional list of priorities to filter by. Accepted values: critical, high, moderate, low.

name: count_notifications_by_status
description:
Count notifications grouped by status.

Optional arguments:
- status: Array of statuses (queued, sent, delivered, opened, failed)

status.description: Optional array of statuses to filter by. Accepted values: queued, sent, delivered, opened, failed.

name: count_notifications_by_incident
description:
Count notifications grouped by incident.

Optional arguments:
- incident_id: Array of incident IDs to filter by

incident_id.description: Optional array of incident IDs to limit the count.

name: average_notifications_by_incident
description:
Calculate the average number of notifications per incident.

Optional arguments:
- incident_id: Array of incident IDs to limit the calculation

name: count_notifications_by_type
description:
Count notifications grouped by type.

Optional arguments:
- type: Array of notification types (report, update, alert, reminder, solution_proposal, other)

type.description: Optional array of notification types to filter by. Accepted values: report, update, alert, reminder, solution_proposal, other.

name: find_notifications_for_email
description:
Retrieve notifications sent to a specific email address.
        
        Use this tool to find all notifications that have been sent to a particular email address.
        
        Parameters:
          - email (required): Email address to search for
        
        Returns a list of notification objects if found, or an empty list if no notifications exist for the email.

name: find_notifications_sent_for_incident
description:
Retrieve notifications related to a specific incident.
        
        Use this tool to find all notifications that have been sent regarding a particular incident.
        
        Parameters:
          - incident_id (required): Incident ID to search for
        
        Returns a list of notification objects if found, or an empty list if no notifications exist for the incident.

name: find_notifications
description:
Search for notifications using various filter criteria.
        
        Use this tool to find notifications based on different filter parameters.
        All parameters below are optional, and you can search without providing any parameters to get all notifications.
        Empty string or null values for any parameter will be ignored in the search.
        
        Parameters:
          - notification_id (optional): Filter by notification ID
          - incident_id (optional): Filter by incident ID
          - email (optional): Filter by recipient email address
          - type (optional): Filter by notification type (alert, update, reminder, report, solution_proposal, other)
          - status (optional): Filter by notification status (queued, sent, delivered, opened, failed)
          - created_before (optional): Filter for notifications created before this timestamp (ISO format)
          - created_after (optional): Filter for notifications created after this timestamp (ISO format)
        
        Returns a list of notification objects that match the criteria.

status.description: Filter by notification status

created_before.description: Filter for notifications created before this timestamp (ISO format)

created_after.description: Filter for notifications created after this timestamp (ISO format)

name: send_notification
description:
Create and send a new notification.
        
        Use this tool to create a new notification associated with an incident.
        
        Parameters:
          - incident_id (required): ID of the associated incident
          - email (required): Email address for notification delivery
          - type (optional): Type of notification (alert, update, reminder, report, solution_proposal, other)
          - status (optional): Status of notification (queued, sent, delivered, opened, failed)
          - subject (optional): Subject of the notification
          - message (optional): Message content of the notification
        
        Returns the created notification object.

status.description: Status of notification

name: update_notification
description:
Update an existing notification.
        
        Use this tool to update the properties of an existing notification.
        
        Parameters:
          - notification_id (required): ID of notification to update
          - incident_id (optional): New incident ID
          - email (optional): New email address
          - type (optional): New notification type (alert, update, reminder, report, solution_proposal, other)
          - status (optional): New notification status (queued, sent, delivered, opened, failed)
          - subject (optional): New subject
          - message (optional): New message content
        
        Returns the updated notification object, or an error if the notification doesn't exist.

status.description: New notification status

name: delete_notifications
description:
Delete a notification by ID.
        
        Use this tool to delete a specific notification by its notification_id.
        The notification must belong to your organization.
        
        Parameters:
          - notification_id (required): The ID of the notification to delete (e.g., NOTIF_001)
        
        Returns a success message with the deleted notification ID.

notification_id.description: The ID of the notification to delete (e.g., NOTIF_001)

name: map_change_request
description:
Create a mapping between a change and either an incident or a problem.

Requirements:
- `change_id` is mandatory.
- At least one of `incident_id` or `problem_id` must be provided (you can supply both to create parallel links).
- The API automatically scopes the mapping to the caller's organization via headers.

Common uses:
- Link an in-flight change to impacted incidents for traceability.
- Associate a change with the problem it resolves.

change_id.description: Change ID (e.g., CHG_001). Must belong to the caller's organization.

incident_id.description: Incident ID to link (optional if problem_id supplied).

problem_id.description: Problem ID to link (optional if incident_id supplied).

name: list_change_request_mappings
description:
List change mappings with powerful filters.

All parameters are optional; omitting filters returns every mapping within the caller's organization.
Useful filters include change_id, incident_id, problem_id, and ISO timestamps for created_at windows.

created_after.description: Return mappings created on/after this ISO timestamp (e.g., 2024-02-10T09:00:00).

created_before.description: Return mappings created on/before this ISO timestamp.

name: find_change_request_mappings_for_incident
description: Retrieve all mappings tied to a specific incident ID within the caller's org.

incident_id.description: Incident ID (INC_###) whose change mappings should be returned.

name: find_change_request_mappings_for_problem
description: Retrieve all mappings tied to a specific problem ID within the caller's org.

problem_id.description: Problem ID (PRB_###) whose change mappings should be returned.

name: delete_change_request_mappings
description:
Delete change request mappings using precise filters with AND logic.

This tool deletes change request mappings based on filter criteria. It requires at least one
filter and uses AND logic when multiple filters are provided (all filters must match).

Safety requirements:
- You **must** provide at least one filter (`change_request_mapping_id`, `change_id`, `incident_id`, or `problem_id`).
- If multiple filters are provided, ALL must match (AND logic) - this ensures precise deletion.
- Prevents accidental bulk deletions by requiring all specified criteria to match.

Returns:
- deleted_count: Number of mappings deleted
- deleted_mappings: List of deleted mapping objects with full details
- message: Success message indicating what was deleted

Examples:
- Delete by mapping ID: {"change_request_mapping_id": "CRM_001"}
- Delete by change and incident: {"change_id": "CHG_001", "incident_id": "INC_001"}
- Delete by change only: {"change_id": "CHG_001"}

incident_id.description: Delete mappings linked to this incident.

problem_id.description: Delete mappings linked to this problem.

name: link_affected_ci_to_incident
description:
Attach a configuration item to an incident, creating a TASKCI_xxx link.

Requirements:
- configuration_item: existing CI ID within the caller's organization
- incident_id: existing incident ID within the same org

name: list_incident_affected_cis
description:
List configuration items linked to incidents. All parameters optional.
Use filters like incident_id or configuration_item to narrow the response, or created_before/after for time windows.

created_before.description: Return mappings created on/before this ISO timestamp.

created_after.description: Return mappings created on/after this ISO timestamp.

name: remove_affected_ci_from_incident
description: Delete incident-CI links. You must provide at least one filter (ID, incident_id, configuration_item, or date range).

created_before.description: Delete mappings created on/before this ISO timestamp

created_after.description: Delete mappings created on/after this ISO timestamp

name: find_incident_slas
description:
List Incident SLA records with optional filters.
All parameters are optional; omitting them returns every incident SLA for the caller's organization.

stage.description: Filter by stage (in_progress, paused, completed, cancelled, breached)

name: find_stage_wise_breached_incident_sla_counts
description:
Return the breached incident SLA counts grouped by stage.
Provide an optional array of stages (["in_progress", "breached", ...]) to limit the aggregation.

name: link_new_incident_sla
description:
Create a new Incident SLA record (incident_sla).
Requires incident_id, sla_def_id, and start_time. Stage defaults to IN_PROGRESS.

name: update_incident_sla_details
description: Update an existing Incident SLA. Provide the incident_sla_id plus one or more fields to change.

stage.description: New stage (in_progress, paused, completed, cancelled, breached)

name: delete_incident_slas
description:
Delete Incident SLA records filtered by identifiers or timestamps.
At least one filter is required (incident_sla_id, incident_id, sla_def_id, stage, has_breached, created_before, created_after).
